body: PT Trinitan Green Energy Minerals (TTEM) sold shares of its subsidiaries, PT Hydortech Metal Indonesia and PT Gostal Nickel Indonesia, to PT Tgem Pt Ltd Singapore for IDR 15.70 billion, aiming to improve its balance sheet, which currently shows total assets of IDR 672.04 billion, total liabilities of IDR 726.81 billion, and an equity deficiency of IDR 54.77 billion. The company's revenue dropped to IDR 9.63 billion in December 2022 from IDR 15.70 billion in December 2021, with a gross loss of IDR 20.28 billion and a net loss of IDR 66.72 billion.
title: PT Trinitan Green Energy Minerals sells subsidiary shares to improve balance sheet.

body: Indonesia's inflation rate in October 2024 decreased to 1.71%, lower than the previous year's 2.56% and the lowest in three years, with annual inflation contributions from various expenditure groups including food, housing, and healthcare. The cumulative inflation rate from the beginning of the year to October 2024 was 0.82%, down from 1.80% in October 2023 and 4.73% in October 2022.
title: Indonesia's inflation rate drops to 1.71% in October 2024, lowest in three years.

body: This November, several Indonesian companies will settle their bond debts, including PT Bank Rakyat Indonesia Tbk (BRI) with IDR 2.17 trillion, PT Indosat Tbk with IDR 21 billion, and PT Adira Dinamika Multi Finance Tbk with IDR 834.39 billion, among others. The total value of these bond payments amounts to IDR 5.43 trillion, with maturity dates ranging from November 7 to November 27.
title: Several Indonesian companies to settle bond debts worth trillions of rupiah in November.

body: PT Bank Rakyat Indonesia Tbk (BBRI) reported a 8.2% year-on-year growth in loan financing distribution to IDR 1.35 quadrillion in September 2024, with a significant improvement in credit quality, as non-performing loans shrank below 3%. The bank's third-party funds also increased by 4.1% year-on-year to IDR 1.36 quadrillion, with current account savings accounts dominating 64.2% of the funds.
title: Bank Rakyat Indonesia's loan financing distribution grows 8.2% year-on-year to IDR 1.35 quadrillion in September 2024.

body: The State Ministry of State-Owned Enterprises has appointed Simon Aloysius Mantiri as President Director of PT Pertamina (PERTM), replacing Nicke Widyawati, as part of a leadership transition that follows existing regulations. The ministry has also appointed Mochammad Iriawan as President Commissioner, Dony Oskaria as Vice President Commissioner, and Raden Adjeng Sondaryani as Independent Commissioner of Pertamina.
title: State Ministry of State-Owned Enterprise Appoints New Leadership at PT Pertamina.

body: PT Pyridam Farma Tbk (PYFA) reported a net loss of IDR 214.2 billion for the first nine months of 2024, a significant increase from IDR 53.21 billion in the same period last year, despite a 123% year-on-year increase in net sales to IDR 1.16 trillion. The company's losses were driven by a significant increase in cost of goods sold, selling and marketing expenses, and general administrative expenses, as well as a loss from foreign exchange.
title: PT Pyridam Farma Tbk reports net loss of IDR 214.2 billion in 9M 2024 despite 123% year-on-year sales growth.

body: Foreign investors purchased 2.85 billion shares and sold 2.98 billion shares, resulting in a net foreign volume of -133.37 million shares, with PT Goto Gojek Tokopedia Tbk (GOTO) being the top buy with 1.06 billion shares purchased and a net volume of 181.64 million shares. On the sell side, PT Bank Rakyat Indonesia Tbk (BBRI) led the way with 129.11 million shares sold, resulting in a net volume of -61.73 million shares.
title: Foreign investors buy and sell nearly 3 billion shares in Indonesian market, with digital company PT Goto Gojek Tokopedia Tbk being the top buy.

body: PT Trimegah Sekuritas Indonesia Tbk (TRIM) acquired 424.80 million shares of PT Energi Mega Persada Tbk, increasing its ownership stake from 14.41% to 16.12%. Other notable transactions include PT Aladin Global Ventures selling 12 million shares of PT Bank Aladin Syariah Tbk, and foreign investors Gochean Holdings and Salween Investment selling 3.8 million shares each of PT Triputra Agro Persada Tbk, resulting in a final ownership percentage of 5.37% and 7.35%, respectively.
title: Multiple companies in Indonesia see changes in ownership stakes following recent share acquisitions and sales.

body: Foreign investors bought a net 205.16 million shares, with PT Bumi Resources Tbk (BUMI) and PT Buana Lintas Lautan Tbk (BULL) being the top two most acquired stocks, with net volumes of 166.77 million and 57.78 million shares, respectively. On the other hand, PT Bumi Resources Minerals Tbk (BRMS) and PT Goto Gojek Tokopedia Tbk (GOTO) were the top two most sold stocks, with net volumes of -155.56 million and -65.39 million shares, respectively.
title: Foreign investors buy 205.16 million more shares than they sell in Indonesian market, led by coal mining and shipping companies.

body: PT Indonesian Paradise Property Tbk (INPP) reported a 121% year-on-year increase in profit to IDR 342.6 billion at the end of September 2024, driven by a 40-fold increase in other operating income and a 45-fold increase in share profits from associated entities. The company's revenue grew 6% year-on-year to IDR 878.1 billion, with the hospitality segment contributing IDR 424.4 billion and the commercial segment contributing IDR 365.3 billion, and INPP is targeting a 20% revenue increase by the end of 2024.
title: Indonesian Paradise Property records 121% year-on-year profit increase in Q3 2024 driven by strong recurring income and growth in hospitality and commercial segments.

body: PT Adira Dinamika Multi Finance Tbk (ADMF) recorded a 9% year-over-year decline in new financing to IDR 27.8 trillion at the end of September 2024, due to a sluggish automotive industry, while its financing receivables grew 7% year-over-year to IDR 56.6 trillion. Despite a revenue increase of 8.89% year-over-year to IDR 7.5 trillion, the company's net profit shrank 17% year-over-year to IDR 1.1 trillion due to increased expenses, particularly allowance for impairment losses.
title: Adira Dinamika Multi Finance Sees 9% Decline in New Financing Amid Sluggish Automotive Industry.

body: PT Timah TBK (TINS) reported a profit of IDR 908.81 billion in Q3 2024, a 169% increase from its previous target, driven by a 9% revenue growth to IDR 8.25 trillion, attributed to a 15% increase in the average selling price of tin metal. The company's tin ore production, metal production, and metal sales also rose by 26%, 25%, and 21%, respectively, with 91% of its products exported to six main countries, including Singapore, South Korea, and the United States.
title: PT Timah's profit surges 169% to IDR 908.81 billion in Q3 2024 driven by increased sales volume and global tin prices.

body: PT Sinar Mas Agro Resources Technology Tbk (SMAR) will distribute an interim dividend of IDR 301.58 billion from its 2024 financial year, equivalent to IDR 105 per share, following a net profit of IDR 1.03 trillion as of September 30, 2024. The dividend payment will be made, with the recording date set for shareholders who are entitled to receive the dividend.
title: PT Sinar Mas Agro Resources Technology to Distribute IDR 301.58 Billion Interim Dividend for 2024 Financial Year.

body: PT Puradelta Lestari Tbk (PDLT) received a IDR 1.5 trillion loan facility from PT Bank Mandiri Tbk (BMRI) to develop the 3,200-hectare Deltamas area, with the credit facility usable within five years and collateralized by land in Kota Deltamas. In Q3 2024, PDLT recorded IDR 1.7 trillion in operating revenues, up 71.8% year-over-year, and IDR 1.12 trillion in net profit, up 84.7% year-over-year.
title: PT Puradelta Lestari receives IDR 1.5 trillion loan from PT Bank Mandiri for Deltamas area development.

body: PT Indika Energy Tbk (INDY) reported a 63.33% year-over-year decline in profit at the end of Q3 2024, driven by a 22.36% decrease in revenue to USD 1.78 billion and increased financial expenses, resulting in a net profit of USD 34.40 million, down from USD 98.83 million in the same period last year. The decline was seen across all segments, including contract services and coal sales, which dropped 17.65% and 23.64% year-over-year, respectively, while other trading activities saw a 36.09% surge.
title: Indika Energy's profit plummets 63.33% in Q3 2024 due to revenue decline and increased financial expenses.

body: PT Intra Golflink Resorts Tbk (NGK) has secured a IDR 500 billion facility from PT Bank Nationanobu Tbk to develop a 6-star hotel in Bali, with IDR 450 billion allocated for construction and IDR 50 billion for working capital and maintenance. The company reported IDR 99.47 billion in revenue and IDR 15.39 billion in net profit in Q3 2024, with the new facility expected to boost future revenue.
title: PT New Kuta Golf Ocean View secures IDR 500 billion facility to develop 6-star hotel and golf course facilities in Bali.

body: PT Astra International Tbk (ASII) recorded a relatively stable profit trend in September 2024, with a 2% year-over-year (YoY) revenue increase to IDR 246.33 trillion, driven by growth in financial services, infrastructure, logistics, and information technology segments. The company's net profit attributable to the parent entity also increased 1% YoY to IDR 25.85 trillion, despite a 9% contraction in car sales and stagnant agribusiness segment performance.
title: Astra Group Records Stable Profit Trend in September 2024 After Previous Slippages

body: PT Primarindo Asia Infrastructure Tbk (PTPA) will divest two land plots and a building in Bandung, West Java, worth IDR 64.23 billion, to pay off its obligations to creditors and suppliers. The proceeds will be allocated to pay IDR 11.78 billion to PT Perusahaan Pengelola Aset and the remaining amount to settle other debts owed to suppliers, employees, and social security administrators.
title: Primarindo Asia Infrastructure to divest land and building assets to pay off creditors and suppliers.

body: PT Sari Kreasi Boga Tbk (RAFI), the holder of the Kebab Baba Rafi franchise license, reported a 76.8% decline in net profit to IDR 24.62 billion in the first nine months of 2024, compared to IDR 106.05 billion in the same period last year. Despite the decline in net profit, the company's revenue soared 21.8% year-on-year to IDR 356 billion, with gross profit margin improving 9.65% from 7.7% in the same period last year.
title: PT Sari Kreasi Boga's Net Profit Plummets 76.8% Despite 21.8% Revenue Growth in First Nine Months of 2024.
